topic: Peta: Scoped Credentials for AI Agents (No Raw API Key Exposure)

key_points: Agents must never decide their own access or see raw credentials
OAuth token storage, automatic refresh, multi-tenant credential isolation
Lightest option for teams wanting clean auth without an opinionated platform
Common risks: overprivileged API keys, no per-agent identity, no revocation mechanism
Static API keys and service accounts break fast in agentic workflows
Recommends per-agent scoped credentials and audit logging
Solution: eliminate secrets altogether using workload identity and dynamic credentials
Runtime context-aware credentials that adapt per execution
Future of secrets management in the era of agentic AI
Scope discipline is critical — avoid broad permissions like admin or write:*
Design scopes at granular level: transactions:read vs transactions
Build custom agent auth layer on top of WorkOS primitives
8 best practices for API security in AI agent systems
RBAC/ABAC with per-user, per-tool capability filtering
Short-lived tokens with automatic rotation and revocation

stats.fact: 28,649,024 new secrets were exposed in public GitHub commits in 2025 — a 34% year-over-year increase, the largest annual jump ever recorded
Over 1.2 million AI-service secrets were exposed in 2025, with 81% year-over-year growth; 12 of the top 15 fastest-growing leaked secret types were AI services
24,008 unique secrets were found exposed in MCP configuration files alone
93% of popular AI agent projects use unscoped API keys with no per-agent identity, no user consent, and no revocation mechanism
OpenRouter credential leaks grew more than 48x year-over-year in 2025, as agents use it as a gateway to multiple models via a single API key
Claude Code co-authored commits leaked secrets at roughly double the baseline rate on public GitHub in 2025
